description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly JavaScript API implementation in DefaultReferenceValue() (src/wasm/wasm-js.cc). The function incorrectly returns JavaScript undefined as the default value for externref (kExtern) and nullexternref (kNoExtern) reference types, instead of returning null. According to the wasm-gc specification, only null values are permitted as default values for reference types. Because undefined is not a valid wasm value, this discrepancy causes type confusion: optimizing compilers (TurboFan, TurboShaft) may falsely treat code paths as unreachable and miscompile them, and a nullexternref holding undefined can be misinterpreted as another reference type such as kExternString. In the reported proof-of-concept, the undefined value is confused into a kExternString with a fake length of 0x7ff80000, allowing out-of-bounds reads via String.prototype.charCodeAt until a guard page is hit, resulting in a segfault. The bug was introduced by commit 2e357c4 in M112 which added wasm null support.

fixes.evidence_text: [https://crrev.com/c/5922878](<https://crrev.com/c/5922878>) approved for merge to M130, please merge to 13.0 at your earliest convenience / before EOD tomorrow 17 October so this fix can be included in the next Stable channel update -- thanks!
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/5922878 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
